quotes.verbatim_quote:
The exercises to follow put into actual play everything you have studied. Study and analyze
them carefully. Pay strict attention to how chords are forced against the melody and, at
some points, even dropped for a better melodic line.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Method's core rule for chord-melody arranging: chords serve the melody, and must be omitted when they conflict.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Symetric Harmony and Counterpoint offer a wealth of ideas to a Jazz musician; I have only
opened the door to these subjects. You would do well to study further these musical
"Giants".
quotes.why_load_bearing: Positions Symmetric Harmony and Counterpoint as the two principal theoretical systems of the method.
Direct cross-reference to a specific harmonic technique applied at a marked point in the score.
Directs student to apply the Cadence Break technique at a specific structural point.
Cross-reference ties a specific moment in the score to the chord-omission rule for the sake of melody.
